articles.title: 20-0: How Spain utterly owned Argentina in World Cup final and rendered Messi irrelevant
articles.description: Spain's triumph was built not on moments of brilliance alone but on collective superiority. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled possession, dictated territory and suffocated every avenue Argentina attempted to explore. World Cup finals are often remembered for drama, controversy or moments of individual inspiration. This one will be remembered for something far rarer - complete domination. Spain didn't just beat the defending champions. They made them disappear.
articles.full_text: Vineet Ramakrishnan is Editor - Sports at Times Internet and has spent over 15 years in digital media. Specialising in cricket and sports content strategy, he occasionally ventures into number-driven storytelling. Away from cricket, he keeps tabs on MMA and the wonderfully chaotic world of pro-wrestling. Still has a soft spot for Lance Klusener's brand of mayhem and firmly believes you can't see John Cena. Mostly reposts on @mid_carder Read More

articles.title: FIFA chief guides Trump away as US president refuses to leave stage - Watch
articles.description: Spain secured their second World Cup title by defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time. US President Donald Trump stood with the Spanish players during the trophy presentation ceremony. Boos were heard around the stadium as Trump and Infantino walked onto the field. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the match. Trump had previously intervened in a red card controversy during the tournament.

articles.title: Arizona mass shooting: 10 injured after gunman opens fire in Tucson
articles.description: A mass shooting in crowded downtown Tucson wounded at least ten people early Sunday. Police officers on patrol heard gunfire and responded to the crowded entertainment district. They encountered and shot the suspected gunman, who sustained life-threatening injuries. Nine other adults were shot and taken to hospitals in critical condition.

articles.title: Larry Ellison once struggled to pay his electricity bills. Now, he has written a $300 million cheque to...
articles.description: Once grappling with utility bills for his workshop, tech magnate Larry Ellison transformed his fortunes by acquiring 98 percent of a Hawaiian island for a staggering $300 million. Now residing full-time on Lanai, he envisions a luxury destination coupled with a sustainable community. Despite fluctuations in his wealth driven by market trends, Oracle's cloud operations showcase remarkable strength amid ongoing challenges.

articles.title: Gold, Silver Rate Today Live Updates: International gold slips as Middle East tensions push crude higher
articles.full_text: The precious metals market is balancing two competing forces, rising geopolitical uncertainty and expectations of tighter monetary policy. Fresh US airstrikes against Iran following attacks on American-linked installations in Jordan have intensified tensions in the Middle East, keeping investors alert. At the same time, analysts say attention remains firmly on the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting and comments from policymakers regarding the interest rate outlook. According to JM Financial, gold's current momentum remains corrective, although developments involving the US and Iran could quickly alter market sentiment. Traders are therefore keeping a close watch on both geopolitical headlines and macroeconomic indicators before taking fresh positions. Physical demand for gold showed signs of weakness across major Asian markets. In India, discounts widened to their highest level in nearly a month as jewellery demand remained muted and many buyers stayed on the sidelines in anticipation of lower prices. Meanwhile, premiums in China were largely unchanged, reflecting subdued retail demand. Analysts said weaker physical buying has added to the pressure on bullion prices, especially at a time when global investors are already cautious due to interest rate expectations and geopolitical developments. Demand trends in India and China are closely watched because the two countries are among the world's largest gold consumers. Market experts believe gold and silver could continue facing headwinds in the near term as investors assess multiple global developments simultaneously. Apart from the escalating conflict in West Asia, traders will also monitor crude oil prices, US weekly jobless claims, flash PMI readings, monetary policy decisions and economic data from Europe and China. Analysts say these indicators will provide fresh clues on inflation trends and the future path of interest rates. According to market participants, bullion is likely to remain sensitive to every major economic release, with investors avoiding aggressive fresh positions until greater clarity emerges. Last week, domestic precious metals also witnessed heavy selling pressure, mirroring weakness in global markets. On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures for August delivery fell by Rs 2,572, or nearly 2%, to close around Rs 1.4 lakh per 10 grams. Silver futures for September delivery dropped even more sharply, declining Rs 6,261, or 2.8%, to settle at Rs 2.16 lakh per kilogram. Analysts said higher crude oil prices, expectations of prolonged elevated interest rates and a firm US dollar weighed on investor sentiment. The rupee's mild correction offered little support, as global factors continued to dominate price movements in domestic markets. Gold ended the previous week with a sharp loss after touching a two-week low, reflecting the dominance of macroeconomic concerns over its traditional safe-haven appeal. Spot gold recorded a weekly decline of 2.5%, while Comex gold futures fell 2.3% during the same period. Analysts attributed the weakness to a combination of a stronger US dollar, rising Treasury yields and expectations that US interest rates could remain higher for longer. Although geopolitical tensions typically support bullion demand, investors continued to book profits instead of building fresh positions, keeping prices under pressure throughout the week. Gold prices opened the week lower as rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East pushed Brent crude oil above the $90-a-barrel mark, reviving concerns that higher energy prices could keep inflation elevated. Spot gold fell 0.4% to $4,000.55 an ounce, while US gold futures for August delivery slipped 0.3% to $4,005.9. At the same time, comments from several US Federal Reserve policymakers indicating that further interest rate hikes may still be needed added pressure on bullion.

articles.title: Oil prices jump 3% as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel
articles.description: Oil prices climbed significantly as United States and Iran conflict intensified. Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz faced increasing pressure and disruptions. US military actions continued for a ninth consecutive night against Iran. Bahrain and Kuwait reported fresh missile and drone strikes across the region. Escalating tensions kept oil prices on an upward trajectory.

articles.title: More brands in store, but price cuts may lag
articles.description: New British brands are expected to enter Indian markets, offering consumers more retail choices. While duty cuts are beneficial, rupee depreciation may limit immediate price reductions. Some brands are pausing price hikes and reducing costs on specific product categories. Global supply chain issues and currency volatility also impact pricing strategies for businesses. Over time, consumers can anticipate more competitive pricing as import duties are further reduced.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: DU BTech Admission 2026 round 3 seat allotment out, July 21 deadline to accept seats: Check direct link here
articles.description: Delhi University has announced the Round 3 seat allotment results for BTech Admissions 2026 on its admission portal. Candidates can check their allocation status by logging into their dashboards using their JEE credentials. Allotted students must accept their seats by July 21, while document verification and fee payment will continue until July 23.

articles.title: AP EAPCET counselling 2026 likely to begin today: Check complete details here
articles.description: The AP EAPCET 2026 Phase 1 counselling registration is set to begin on July 20 for admissions to undergraduate engineering programmes in Andhra Pradesh. Eligible candidates must complete online registration, upload documents, and pay the processing fee by July 29. Only verified applicants will be allowed to participate in the web options and seat allotment process.

articles.title: UPTET Result 2026 expected soon as UPESSC completes final evaluation process
articles.description: UPTET Result 2026 is expected to be announced soon by the Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission. Lakhs of candidates who appeared for the teacher eligibility examination are waiting for their scorecards and merit list PDF. The Commission has not announced the official result date yet. Candidates who qualify will receive a lifetime-valid UPTET certificate. The article covers qualifying marks, exam levels, result checking process and steps to download the merit list.

articles.title: Kerala declares Monday holiday for schools and colleges after FIFA World Cup 2026 final
articles.description: Kerala has declared a holiday for all educational institutions on Monday ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain. The decision was taken considering the match's late-night schedule, which could make it difficult for students to attend morning classes. However, pre-scheduled examinations will continue as planned. The announcement came after demands from opposition leaders and others seeking relief for students. The move highlights Kerala's football enthusiasm and student convenience.

articles.title: 'Ramayana' concept art transformed into FIRST LOOKS - PICS
articles.description: Fans are eagerly awaiting the official trailer launch for the film 'Ramayana' this week. They have creatively transformed concept art into impressive AI-generated character depictions. These fan creations offer glimpses of characters like Sai Pallavi's Sita and Yash's Ravana. The film's makers also unveiled a special comic book detailing the mythology-inspired world. The official trailer will debut online after a grand event at San Diego Comic-Con.

articles.description: National Award winners Mammootty and Dhanush celebrated their recent wins together. Director Rajkumar Periyasamy and editor R. Kalaivanan also joined the joyous occasion. They cut a cake on the sets of their upcoming film titled 'OM'. This collaboration brings together Mammootty and Dhanush for the first time. articles.title: Kiran Abbavaram reacts to 'Obsession' comparison backlash
articles.description: Actor Kiran Abbavaram has addressed the backlash he received over comments made about a film comparison. In a recent podcast, he clarified that his remarks were misinterpreted and only referenced a single plot similarity. He expressed profound respect for the success of the movie 'Obsession,' stressing that his intentions were humorous rather than disrespectful towards other films.
